latest.title: Cows remember faces across the herd
latest.script_key: cows can remember who belongs in the herd watch the calm face first because cows can recognize familiar herd members and people that memory lowers stress when the group feels predictable a quiet look can carry more history than it seems which farm animal should we explain next

latest.title: Forests make cooler air under the canopy
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Rock layers reveal heat moving under the surface
latest.script_key: rock layers can expose heat moving below ground watch the steam because hot water rises through cracks after being heated underground pressure and minerals turn hidden energy into a visible signal at the surface which geothermal clue should come next

latest.title: Dogs build a map with smell
latest.script_key: dogs build a map with smell watch the nose before the feet because every sniff samples layers of scent dogs can learn who passed where they moved and what changed since the last check the ground is basically a news feed what should they sniff next

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: dogs don’t see white snow as white
latest.script_key: this dog s snowy world isn t white dogs only see blue and yellow so snow looks like pale green that s why they tilt their head trying to figure out the blur would your dog pass a vision test

latest.title: Cows remember every face they meet
latest.script_key: look at the cow s ears twitching left then right that s how it scans your face without moving its head cows not only recognize humans they remember moods happy or upset from years ago they re basically running a mental rolodex out there that s why they sometimes act like they hate a farmer who switched shirts

latest.title: Gorillas eat leaves to survive the rainforest
latest.script_key: this gorilla survives on leaves notice how it strips the tough edges off before eating that s because its gut bacteria ferment the fibers so it can digest them a silverback needs 40 pounds of leaves a day that s why it spends half the day chewing would you survive on such a diet

latest.title: Deer can aim each ear toward danger
latest.script_key: deer can aim each ear toward danger watch the ears first because deer can turn them to sample sound from different directions that quick listening check helps them decide whether to freeze flee or keep feeding one quiet second tells the whole body what to do would you freeze

latest.title: Eastern screech owl hides in plain sight
latest.script_key: that face isn t staring at you notice the ear tufts blend into the bark like broken twigs screech owls don t spin their heads 360 their eyes are fixed that s why they tilt their whole bodies side to side like a bobblehead did you ever think they were just bad at turning around

latest.title: Whales don't sink because they've got balloons
latest.script_key: whales can t sink if they try because their lungs are like built in floaties watch this one twice their blowhole sprays water but those lungs can hold air like a scuba tank that s why these 60 ton giants just chill on the surface did you learn this wrong too

latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next
